title: Chapter Thirty-Seven: The Monk's Grudge
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

The wine settled warmly through his middle dantian. New true energy filled spaces he had not known were empty. He operated the Jeong Family Dynamic Technique while drinking and observed his body with care.

No change at the baihui acupoint. The crown remained as it was.

*So that's why.*

Distance from hope. This was why he never rested his heart on the Desolate Sect Lord's warmth. Though he had not expected much, strength left his whole body. The middle dantian's gain was real, but the mood sank despite it.

He bowed to the assembled guests. "Thank you to all the heroes who watched over the coming of age ceremony."

The crowd offered their comments as he descended the platform.

"Standing alone without relying on family? The bearing of a great master."

"He'll establish his own sect someday."

He passed Ma Yeon-jeok, whose eyes trembled. He stopped at Ma Se-in's feet. The boy knelt still, his face hollow with futility.

*He submitted completely. Without that, he couldn't have discussed succession. And I brushed it off with a single drink.*

"You," Yeon-shin said quietly. "You seem like you'll be a good leader."

Ma Se-in flinched. Yeon-shin walked on.

Outside the banquet hall, Ma Jin was waiting. Yeon-shin confirmed they were alone.

"Squad leader, are you not married?"

"What?"

"You're the Desolate Ma Family's direct eldest son. In your prime. Yet they're making a fuss about succession. It doesn't make sense."

Ma Jin's scar twisted. He seemed overwhelmed. Then Cheong Myeong appeared at Yeon-shin's side, grinning.

"Shouldn't Lightning Flash know? I'll tell you."

Ma Jin turned his back and walked away. Cheong Myeong waited until the distance was sufficient.

"When the squad leader was white-level, he returned from a mission in Xinjiang holding Mi-ryeo's hand. She was a small child then. They brought her from a secretly transmitted sect. But Xinjiang is a battlefield where remnants of the annihilated Demonic Cult run wild."

He paused. His usual hanging smile faded.

"The squad leader volunteered for the mission. Completed it. But during a fight with an internal force master..." Cheong Myeong sighed. "He was hit with an internal force technique. A vital point. He kicked at the face, but the enemy dodged and struck underneath."

"A vicious man," Yeon-shin murmured.

"Several seniors on that mission became squad leaders. Even the Azury Sky Lord who doesn't get along won't mention that incident."

"I understand."

"Ma Jin hasn't been able to function as a man since then. That's why Mi-ryeo serves the squad leader as a benefactor."

*The martial world is a harsh place.* He pictured the young Ma Jin. A white-level boy fighting beyond his depth.

"Kunlun Sect's Cloud Dragon Great Eight Forms," Yeon-shin said. "Shaolin's Buddha Light Energy Flow. Body techniques that can move in empty air. I'd like to witness them."

Cheong Myeong smiled. "The next mission is in Henan. We'll cooperate with Shaolin. If luck follows, you might see the Buddha Light Energy Flow."

---

A month's rest after the ceremony. It was meaningless.

Yeon-shin wanted to go on missions without pause.

"It might be good to secure a position in Henan early," Ma Jin said from behind his desk in the office, accepting Yeon-shin's usual intensity with the discomfort of a man who knew he could not slow it.

"What exactly is the mission?"

"Annihilation. We cut off one of the Blood Flame Sect's arms. If luck follows, we learn about their main headquarters." He took a sip of tea. "While you were catching Thieving Ghost, there was an apostle who engaged with me. One whose hair had completely turned black. A monster surpassing the Blood Swordmasters. We've tracked his movements."

"From Shaanxi to Henan?"

"His trail ended at Mount Pingding in Henan. That's Shaolin Temple territory."

"Shaolin is at Mount Song in Zhengzhou. That's quite a distance."

"They're the first orthodox sect. All martial arts under heaven come from Shaolin. Few sects in the Central Plains haven't been influenced by their techniques. You can consider all of Henan Province under Shaolin's influence."

Ma Jin turned his gaze while mentioning the province. Yeon-shin nodded. His uncle knew where the Jeong family estate had stood, and the discomfort showed in every careful glance.

"Radiant Demon First Team and Second Team will go with me," Ma Jin continued.

First Team. Yeon-shin, Hyeon Won-chang, Cheong Myeong, Baek Mi-ryeo. Reorganised after the Great Violet Pill mission for better coordination.

"Does our team not need to rest?"

"Everyone is like you. Either they enjoy achieving merit or they enjoy travelling the murim."

*The former is me and Baek Mi-ryeo. The latter is Hyeon Won-chang and Cheong Myeong.*

He left the office. Outside, crimson shadows stretched across the sky. He went to his room without stopping by the training ground.

He lay in bed. Boisterous singing and clinking cups drifted from the other rooms. Sword sounds from the training ground. Laughter washing away exhaustion.

Sunlight blurred through the paper window and brushed his face. Sunset at the day's end.

The crown grew hot. It was always like this around this time. When had it started? The upper dantian invited nature's energy to dance within him, and the more it played, the wider the door opened. Regardless of his will.

The laughter grew louder. The seniors were having fun. He imagined shouting at them to stop.

He closed his eyes. Blue light shone into the blackness behind his lids. For a moment it felt like cold water poured over his skull. All his senses snapped back to reality.

No one was watching. He curled up beneath the covers and crouched there, arms around his knees.

*One more mission. Then blue-level.*

The final mission before promotion lay ahead.

---

The wind hit his face in sheets as the horse beneath him stretched into a gallop. Yeon-shin glanced around from the saddle.

Eight riders flanked him. Nine Radiant Demon Wing masters -- an unprecedented number for a single mission.

*Evidence that this matter is large.*

A mighty company. The drumming of hooves filled the road between Hubei and Henan, scattering farmers and pedlars from their path. The Desolate Sect uniforms drew stares from every village they passed through -- not fear, but a kind of relief. They said the sect earned greater public sentiment than most government inspectors. Because they broke the swords of martial artists who threatened people.

They entered the marketplace of Xinye County. Ma Jin's voice carried, thick with authority, imbued with a thread of true energy that made it ring clear above the hoofbeats.

"Don't know if it's an inn or tavern. We'll rest there."

Passersby who had been walking with anxious faces brightened at the sight of Desolate Sect uniforms. The group stopped before the largest building in the county seat -- a two-storey tavern with a shoddy frame but a wide interior.

Hyeon Won-chang dismounted and looked around. "Is this Xinye County? It's different from what I heard."

To reach Henan from Desolate Sect, they had to pass through Xinye. They had already ridden past the site where the Jeong family estate had stood.

Completely ruined. Not a single tree on the back mountain. The earth was scorched where the forests had been, and the foundation stones of the manor lay scattered like broken teeth along the hillside.

*They burned everything. Just as Thieving Ghost said.*

Baek Mi-ryeo glanced at Yeon-shin's expressionless face. He did not look back. The door inside him rattled -- the same door that held back the courtyard, the bodies, his father's face -- but he kept walking. One foot before the other. Expressionless.

"With the original occupants gone, black path groups would run rampant," Baek Mi-ryeo said quietly.

Then voices spilled from the tavern, carrying through the curtained entrance.

"With the Jeong family gone, it's paradise. The income from those forests--"

"The Blood Flame Sect did us a favour."

"A coward like you learning blood techniques? Ridiculous!"

Yeon-shin's brows drew together.

*Income from the forests.*

The day he had left for Desolate Sect came to mind. The administrator had promised to manage things. That income did not belong to black path thugs.

"Let's go in," he said.

Even the Radiant Demon Wing masters followed their youngest member's lead, watching his mood. When they pushed through the curtains, the noise inside stopped.

Bald men with axes. Thugs flipping daggers. Open-shirted fighters with courtesans on their knees. Black path martial artists from a dozen different places -- not one familiar face.

Yeon-shin stepped forward.

"You lot. Explain about the Jeong family's land."

"What?"

The man who snickered had a word forming on his lips -- *youngster*, probably. Then Hyeon Won-chang reached past Yeon-shin's shoulder and turned the hem of his robe forward.

The *Hwang* character. The emblem of Desolate Sect.

The snicker died. Murmuring filled the tavern.

"Say what you want to say," Yeon-shin said.

---
